provenance: Curated conformance fixtures for the covered browser-vs-net/url divergence set. Rows with source=wpt derive from web-platform-tests url/resources/urltestdata.json @ 181476aa16e8 (2026-07-05); the wpt field records the browser truth (hostname, or FAIL) those rows pin against. Rows with source=hand cover the address-bar classes (outside the URL spec) and the deliberate model boundaries. Expectations are urlform's facts, hand-vetted against the WHATWG reading - NOT regenerated from the implementation. On mismatch, re-derive the expectation from the spec before touching the row. Drift maintenance: periodically re-check the spec and the WPT corpus for changes to the covered set and refresh the commit pin - see the header comment in fixtures_test.go.

cases.name: single-slash http recovers the authority
cases.note: WHATWG special-authority-ignore-slashes: one slash after a special scheme still reads an authority

cases.name: zero-slash http recovers the authority
cases.note: zero slashes: net/url parses an opaque path, the browser reads the authority

cases.name: zero-slash userinfo authority recovers host and flag
non-special scheme recovers nothing
cases.note: the browser reads an opaque path for a non-special scheme; fabricating host evidence here would be a false fact

cases.name: file single-slash recovers nothing
cases.note: file is special for slash handling but its slash forms read an empty host; excluded from authority recovery

cases.name: backslash authority canonicalizes for a special scheme
backslashes inside a special-scheme path canonicalize
non-special backslashes stay opaque
cases.note: scoping fix: a global backslash rewrite would fabricate host evidence 'opaque' a browser never sees

cases.name: query and fragment backslashes are ordinary characters
cases.note: the WHATWG slash equivalence ends at the query; host facts are unaffected either way

cases.name: embedded tab in the host is removed
cases.wpt: preprocessing: remove all ASCII tab or newline
cases.note: the tab-smuggling divergence that motivated the preprocessing; net/url alone rejects the control byte

cases.name: embedded tab in the path is removed
newline-split scheme reassembles
cases.note: removal runs before the parse in the spec's basic parser, so a newline split anywhere - even inside the scheme - reassembles; a string-level scheme allowlist on the raw input would have been bypassed

cases.name: leading C0 controls and trailing controls trim
cases.note: WHATWG edge rule: strip leading/trailing C0-or-space; net/url alone rejects the control bytes

cases.name: interior non-tab C0 control stays malformed
cases.note: aligned boundary: the browser also fails on a C0 control inside a host; only tab/newline are removed

cases.name: port-only special authority recovers no host
cases.note: the browser fails outright, so exposing no host evidence matches; the port fact is reported for the consumer

cases.name: out-of-range port is reported, not rejected
cases.note: DELIBERATE model boundary: urlform reports the fact and the publisher validates the range (Port doc); the browser rejects

cases.name: opaque host-as-scheme recovers nothing
cases.note: 'animebytes.tv' parses as a non-special scheme; the URL-spec reading is an opaque path, and only an address bar would fix it up - evidence stays hidden, consumers fail closed

cases.name: mailto opaque form carries no userinfo fact
cases.note: the @ sits in an opaque path, not an authority; HasUserInfo stays false

cases.name: schemeless host reads as the address bar would
homograph host survives without IDNA mapping
cases.note: DELIBERATE model boundary: no IDNA/UTS46 transform ever runs, so non-ASCII evidence survives for the fail-closed IsASCIIHost gates
